text: This study presents a systematic literature review (SLR) exploring the research trends of STEM and STEAM education within the Indonesian context from 2016 to 2021. Utilizing the PRISMA framework and the Paper Classification Form (PCF), 63 peer-reviewed articles from national and international databases were analyzed. The findings reveal a significant upward trend in publication volume over the last six years, with the majority of studies employing experimental research methods. Learning strategies emerged as the most investigated topic, primarily focused on science subjects at the secondary school level. Results indicate that project-based learning (PjBL), problem-based learning (PBL), and inquiry-based models are the most prevalent frameworks used to integrate STEM/STEAM into the classroom. Furthermore, the analysis identifies critical thinking, scientific literacy, and general learning outcomes as the primary themes associated with this educational approach. The review concludes that while STEM education is maturing in Indonesia, there is a substantial need for more holistic empirical research into STEAM, specifically regarding the role of the arts and the use of advanced educational technologies like robotics and gamification to foster 21st-century skills.
